name: papaver somniferum
intensity: High and dose-dependent due to opioid alkaloid content.
safetyNotes: Avoid with respiratory issues; Avoid in liver disease; Avoid in pregnancy; Avoid with opioid use; Avoid with respiratory disorders; May interact with MAOIs; Avoid with addiction history; Avoid use during pregnancy unless advised by a clinician, may cause high risk if misused, addiction potential, constipation, respiratory depression, dependence, high, human extrapolated), restricted, high-risk profile includes dependence, overdose, and respiratory depression potential, analgesic and sedative; Avoid with respiratory issues or may interact with MAOIs; Avoid use during pregnancy unless advised by a clinician, do not combine with serotonergic drugs or MAOIs without medical guidance or may interact with MAOIs.

sources.title: PubMed search: Papaver somniferum L.
sources.note: Literature index for pharmacology and safety evidence

description: Papaver somniferum is a medicinal herb traditionally used for euphoria and analgesia.
legalStatus: Strictly regulated; illegal to grow for opium in most countries
region: Originally from the Mediterranean and western Asia, now cultivated in multiple regulated regions.
